text: Outcomes are robust to the single LLM judge. From existing transcripts (no new LLM calls): for the Lean studies the LLM judge is non-decisive -- it demotes 0/17 compiler-solved cells and cannot rescue any non-compiling cell, so the solved* count is fixed entirely by the deterministic compiler + zero-sorry check (judge vs compiler-oracle kappa=0.053 only because they target statement-fidelity vs proof-completeness, not because they disagree on solves). For the false-claim studies the lenient verdict-only grader and the strict canonical-regex grader agree on all 72 false-variant cells (kappa=1.000), so the modality-equivalence headline (visual 23/24 = textual 24/24 = reflection 23/24) is grader-invariant. The cross-study conclusions do not hinge on the LLM judge. Caveat: this is judge-vs-objective-oracle, not the pre-registered human-vs-judge kappa, which still requires running grade.py (all your_grade fields are empty).

comments._self: CAVEAT (audit): the false-claim-study kappa=1.000 is agreement-BY-CONSTRUCTION, not independent corroboration. The strict canonical-regex grader is (lenient verdict-only AND a regex) -- i.e. a SUBSET of the lenient grader -- so on cells the lenient grader marks false it can only agree; perfect kappa is near-tautological. a-0015 confirms this directly: its proxy-oracle check finds study5 kappa=1.000 / study6 n/a 'because there the oracle equals the grader by construction (self-consistent, not an independent check)', and the only genuinely independent comparison (study3 compiler-oracle vs LLM fidelity judge) is kappa=0.065. So the modality-equivalence headline being 'grader-invariant' is robust to a grader that cannot disagree, not to an independent grader. Independent robustness still requires the pre-registered human-vs-judge kappa (grade.py unrun; a-0015 overall proxy kappa=0.277, below the 0.6 bar).
